description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

Watch Out for Suspicious Emails and Messages

Scam emails have been identified that impersonate Hanno City Hall's tax department (for example, claiming to be from "City Hall Resident Tax Division") and pressure residents to pay resident income tax using code payment services by clicking on links in the message.

Hanno City does not send emails, SMS, LINE messages, or other communications requesting payment of taxes or overdue charges.

If you receive any suspicious emails, SMS, LINE messages, or similar that you don't recognize, delete them immediately.

• If there is a URL or link in an email, never click on it.

• Even if you do click on it by accident, do not enter any personal information or proceed with payment at the destination site.

• If you have already made a payment, contact your nearest police station right away.

For police inquiries, call the Police Consultation Hotline "#9110" (Government Online)

Be careful of suspicious emails and phone calls (Japan National Tax Agency)

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/somubu/shuzeika/13555.html
summary: ℹ️ Warning: Watch out for scam emails claiming to be about resident income tax payments!
